accountability_rule.summary: AI-assisted work must remain accountable to a human owner and governed role without attributing authorship, contribution, review, validation, approval, release, or maintenance to assistance.

required_evidence: task contract or remediation phase reference
approval evidence when required by path, role, or approval class

positive_requirements: Confirm the accountability record identifies both a human owner and an acting governed role.
Confirm approval evidence is present when required by path, role, or approval-class policy.
negative_requirements: Confirm no prohibited attribution credit appears in governed delivery surfaces.
Confirm assistance is not listed as author, contributor, reviewer, validator, approver, releaser, or maintainer.
Confirm commit metadata, pull request metadata, changelog, release notes, README notices, contributor lists, authorship metadata, and documentation prose do not credit assistance.

rejection_conditions.description: The change credits assistance on a prohibited surface.
The accountability record does not identify both an accountable human owner and an acting governed role.
The accountability record lacks required review or validation evidence.
The accountability record lacks approval evidence required by path, role, or approval-class policy.
